channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: How Your Garden Fence Reflects Your Taste and Neighbourhood Status
item.description: Fences are more than mere boundary markers; they reveal a lot about homeowners’ style and social signals. Traditional options like lap panels are practical and budget-friendly, commonly seen in everyd...

item.title: Post-election Violence and Political Statements in West Bengal After BJP Victory
item.description: After the Bharatiya Janata Party won the West Bengal Assembly elections, tensions escalated as the Trinamool Congress (TMC) alleged that one of its workers was murdered by BJP members in Nanoor, Birbh...

item.title: Philippine and Global Headlines: Flood Control, Inflation, Hantavirus, and Met Gala Updates
item.description: Today’s news highlights both national and international events affecting the Philippines. Senator Panfilo Lacson defended the Senate blue ribbon committee’s flood control probe report despite lacking ...

item.title: Multan Police Investigate Murder of Woman and Three Children Staged as Suicide
item.description: In Multan, a tragic incident occurred where a woman and her three children were found dead in their home. Initially reported as a suicide, police later confirmed that the deaths were murders and the s...

item.title: DMK reacts to Congress's potential alliance with actor Vijay
item.description: The news article reports that the DMK, a prominent political ally in Tamil Nadu, has expressed strong opinions regarding the Congress party's reported plans to form an alliance with actor Vijay. Altho...

item.title: Pakistan Navy Saves Indian and Indonesian Crew from Disabled Ship in Arabian Sea
item.description: On Tuesday, the Pakistan Navy successfully carried out a rescue operation in the Arabian Sea, assisting seven crew members stranded on the offshore vessel MV Gautam. The distressed ship, en route from...

item.title: Hawks’ Handling of Cocaine Evidence Questioned at Madlanga Commission
item.description: During proceedings at the Madlanga Commission of Inquiry, Major-General Hendrick Flynn, head of Serious Organised Crime at the SAPS Directorate for Priority Crime Investigation (Hawks), criticised the...

item.title: Spring Flooding Forces Closures and Damage on Hundreds of Saskatchewan Rural Roads
item.description: Spring flooding is causing widespread damage across Saskatchewan, with hundreds of rural roads affected and several communities declaring local emergencies. The RM of Paddockwood, north of Prince Albe...

item.title: President Ruto Uses Humor on Kiswahili History During Tanzania Visit
item.description: On Monday, May 4, 2026, President William Ruto visited Tanzania and was welcomed warmly, highlighting ongoing development projects between the two countries. During his address to Tanzanian MPs on May...

item.title: Comprehensive Review of Australian Supermarket Sauerkrauts
item.description: A group of eight tasters, led by Nicholas Jordan, conducted a blind taste test of 21 different sauerkraut products available in Australian supermarkets. The objective was to evaluate the acidity, text...

item.title: Woman dies in Bristol house explosion following forced entry by ex-partner
item.description: A tragic incident occurred in Bristol where a 35-year-old woman lost her life after an explosion at her residence. Reports indicate that the explosion was linked to her ex-partner, who had forcibly en...

item.title: Irish Comedy Thrives on Screen with Dark Humour and Mystery
item.description: Ireland’s history of conflict and division has long influenced its film and television productions, often inspiring heavy dramas like 'Angela’s Ashes' and Jim Sheridan’s films such as 'In the Name of ...

item.title: Nairobi Sets Up New Alcohol Licensing Board to Strengthen Enforcement
item.description: Nairobi County Government has launched the induction of its newly constituted Alcoholic Drinks Control and Licensing Board, aiming to bring more order and professionalism into the city’s liquor sector...

item.title: Ontario ticket resale cap challenges season ticket holders’ ability to recover costs
item.description: A new Ontario law that caps ticket resale prices at face value, plus applicable taxes and service fees, is drawing criticism from season ticket holders who say it makes it harder to afford their subsc...

item.title: PNP intensifies monitoring and private partnership after Quezon City pawnshop robbery attempt
item.description: The Philippine National Police (PNP) is stepping up its efforts to monitor high-risk areas and enhance collaboration with business establishments after successfully preventing an attempted robbery at ...

item.title: Tasmanian Piano Tuner Stars in Documentary Premiering at Sydney Film Festival
item.description: Martin Tucker, a Tasmanian piano tuner, has spent over forty years tuning an estimated 18,000 pianos across Australia. His craft is celebrated in the documentary 'The Piano Tuner' by Natalia Laska, wh...

item.title: Antibiotic Resistance Poses Major Threat to Modern Healthcare
item.description: Antibiotics have been one of the most important discoveries in medical history, transforming once-deadly infections into treatable conditions and enabling modern healthcare procedures. However, bacter...

item.title: West Bengal Chief Minister Declines to Resign Despite BJP Electoral Victory
item.description: In a surprising political development, West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee has refused to step down following her party, the Trinamool Congress (TMC), losing the state elections to Prime Minist...

item.title: Simba and Young Africans Keep Title Hopes Alive After 2-2 Draw
item.description: Dar es Salaam saw a thrilling Mainland Premier League derby on Sunday as Simba and Young Africans shared spoils in a 2-2 draw. Young Africans still lead the table with 48 points, five ahead of Simba, ...

item.title: Philippines Hosts Inaugural ASEAN Bar Leaders’ Summit to Strengthen Regional Legal Cooperation
item.description: The Philippines hosted the first ASEAN Bar Leaders’ Summit in Manila, bringing together bar associations from Brunei, Cambodia, Indonesia, Malaysia, Singapore, Vietnam, and the Philippines. Organized ...
